title: Rohit Lalit Killed me in cold blood
description:
Simran Chhabra, a 22 years old young woman went to the hospital of the accused Rohit Lalit alias Langda with the symptoms of cough and cold. Merely for the greed of Rs 200/- which the accused Rohit Lalit, charged for an injection, gave her Dynapar (Diclofenac Symptoms), intravenously. The injection reacted within seconds, that caused her painful death within few minutes.

Accused Rohit Lalit alias langda, instead of seeking apology from the family for his acts, did every possible thing to hide his crime. He bulldozed the family, corrupted the police, filed false FIR against the relatives of Simran and worst he also made attempts on my life.

As the day stands, we have been able to get the trial initiated against the accused Rohit Lalit @ Langda but in the gross abuse of the process of law, the High Court has put a stay on the trial based on false statements.

We strongly believe someday, the Judges  of the High Court will have some better sense and remove the stay.
outcome: The order for trial u/s 304A has been initiated. Presently it has been granted stay by High Court. We are knocking the doors of Supreme Court to get that stay vacated. Undoubtedly, the judges of higher courts are sitting high above in their ivory towers, but together we must scream so loud, that sitting on the pedestal so high, they will some day listen.

title: Brother's 10 years fight to seek justice
description:
I am Mahesh Sharma.
This is the story of my younger brother, Dharmendra, and my decade long fight for justice.

Dharmendra was only thirty-seven years old. He was healthy, disciplined, and lived a simple life. He never smoked and never drank alcohol. In 2012, he complained of mild pain in his abdomen. The doctors said it was only small kidney stones. 

He was treated for some time, and later surgery was advised. The surgery was delayed because the doctor was shifting from Columbia Asia, Gurgaon to Max Hospital. He also said that there are superior machines available at Max. 

On 4 April 2013, Dharmendra was admitted to Max Hospital in Gurgaon. The surgery took place the next day, on 5 April 2013, at around 12:40 in the afternoon.

It was supposed to be a small, keyhole surgery. We were told it would take one or two hours. But surgery continued to beyond 4:00 PM. We were told the surgery was over, yet my brother did not come out. We waited until 7:45 in the evening.

When he was finally brought out, his urine bag was filled with blood. My mother panicked and asked the nurses about the blood. They said, "isme aisa hi hota hai." That night at about 1:30 AM, Dharmender complained about severe pain. He kept calling the doctor for help. The doctor instead of coming or diagnosing the cause of the pain, gave heavy painkillers and  made him unconscious.

By the next morning, my brother was unconscious and no one attended him. Finally at about 9:30 AM, the ICU team arrived. Later at 6:00 PM they diagnosed that there was internal bleeding and about 600 ml of blood was collected. This within a day caused the infection. Next day, they removed the kidney but infection kept on spreading, then they removed his small intestine also. Later they said that the infection has spread to the brain and they will surgically open the skull. 

By then, nothing could be reversed. He remained alive, but only on machines, until 20 June 2013. That day, he finally left us. 

After his death, my real struggle began.

It took immense effort to get an FIR registered. Even then, without a medical board report, nothing moved. In 2014, a medical board report came. It was vague, but it did say that negligence had occurred. Sadly, my lawyer at that time gave me poor advice and pushed me to approach the medical council too early.

The medical council completely cleared the doctor. I learned, the hard way, how corrupt all our institutions are.

For ten long years, the case dragged on. I faced delays, apathy, and constant humiliation. I learned what it truly means to be a victim in the criminal justice system.

As I write this, the trial is over. Final arguments are complete. We are waiting for the judgment.

Please pray that Dahrmender gets the justice and his soul can finally rest in peace.
